title: Promote Python completion only after inventory proof

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Python is promoted only when generated package Python output is the shipped executable projection, command-generation has no package-specific runtime behavior, package source has no parser, help, dispatch, or executable CLI ownership, every generated operation is portable-codegen-primitive-executed or an explicit accepted package-domain primitive with non-portability proof, no Tier 2 primitive hides generic deterministic behavior, and source, installed, and Docker conformance pass.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The slice completes the final Python completion promotion by making remaining package-domain runtime source and generated facade bridges explicit accepted permanent non-generic boundaries, while keeping TypeScript and shell codegen epics out of scope.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Accepted runtime boundaries are recorded in python_runtime_projection_inventory; the completion checker rejects unaccepted source/facade bridges and generic-deterministic-runtime-debt; generated package proof and broad validation passed.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen if python-completion-blockers reports blockers again, if a new generic deterministic runtime boundary appears in accepted package-domain source, or if generated command modules import package runtime helpers directly.
